_comment_overview: GCP MCP Server Configuration Template — Layer 3 (Platform)
Google Cloud offers fully-managed remote MCP servers for a growing set of services.
As of March 2026, official managed MCP servers exist for: BigQuery, Cloud Run,
Compute Engine, GKE, Cloud SQL, AlloyDB, Spanner, Firestore, Bigtable,
Cloud Logging, Cloud Monitoring, Resource Manager, and Google SecOps.
However, many data engineering services (Dataflow, Cloud Composer, Pub/Sub,
Cloud Functions, Vertex AI Pipelines) do NOT yet have official MCP servers.
For those, use the gcloud-mcp CLI wrapper or direct gcloud/API via Bash tool.
Authentication: Google Cloud managed MCP servers use OAuth 2.0 + IAM.
Run 'gcloud auth application-default login' before starting Claude Code.
Enable MCP on each service: gcloud beta services mcp enable SERVICE --project=PROJECT_ID
IMPORTANT: Adapt this template to your engagement. Remove servers you do not need.
Only include servers for services actively used in the project.

mcpServers._comment_official_managed: --- OFFICIAL GOOGLE CLOUD MANAGED MCP SERVERS (remote, OAuth 2.0) ---

_comment: Official Google Cloud BigQuery MCP server (managed, remote).
Provides: schema inspection, query execution, table/dataset listing, job management.
Prerequisite: gcloud beta services mcp enable bigquery.googleapis.com --project=PROJECT_ID
Auth: Uses application-default credentials. Run 'gcloud auth application-default login'.
Docs: https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/use-bigquery-mcp
Official Google Cloud Run MCP server (managed, remote).
Provides: service listing, deployment management, revision inspection, traffic splitting.
Prerequisite: gcloud beta services mcp enable run.googleapis.com --project=PROJECT_ID
Docs: https://docs.cloud.google.com/run/docs/reference/mcp
Official Google Cloud Logging MCP server (managed, remote).
Provides: log entry queries, log bucket management, sink configuration, log scopes.
Prerequisite: gcloud beta services mcp enable logging.googleapis.com --project=PROJECT_ID
Official Google Cloud Monitoring MCP server (managed, remote).
Provides: metric descriptors, time series data, alert policies, error groups.
Prerequisite: gcloud beta services mcp enable monitoring.googleapis.com --project=PROJECT_ID
Official Google Compute Engine MCP server (managed, remote).
Provides: instance listing, VM management, image/disk inspection.
Prerequisite: gcloud beta services mcp enable compute.googleapis.com --project=PROJECT_ID
Official Google Kubernetes Engine MCP server (managed, remote).
Provides: cluster listing, node pool management, workload inspection, Kubernetes API access.
Prerequisite: gcloud beta services mcp enable container.googleapis.com --project=PROJECT_ID
Official Google Cloud Resource Manager MCP server (managed, remote).
Provides: project listing, folder/org inspection, IAM policy queries.
Prerequisite: gcloud beta services mcp enable cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com --project=PROJECT_ID
Official Google gcloud CLI MCP wrapper (community, stdio).
Wraps the gcloud CLI for natural-language interaction with ANY GCP service.
This is the FALLBACK for services without dedicated MCP servers.
Use this for: Dataflow, Cloud Composer, Pub/Sub, Cloud Functions, Vertex AI, IAM, etc.
Source: https://github.com/googleapis/gcloud-mcp
Install: npm install -g @google-cloud/gcloud-mcp
Official Google Cloud Storage MCP server (community, stdio).
Provides: object CRUD, bucket management, IAM policy queries, GCS Insights.
Source: https://github.com/googleapis/gcloud-mcp (storage-mcp package)
Install: npm install -g @google-cloud/storage-mcp
Official Google Cloud Observability MCP server (community, stdio).
Alternative to managed Logging/Monitoring servers — richer toolset.
Provides: log entries, metrics, traces, error groups, alert policies.
Source: https://github.com/googleapis/gcloud-mcp (observability-mcp package)
Install: npm install -g @google-cloud/observability-mcp
Community BigQuery MCP server — read-only, lightweight alternative.
Use when you only need schema inspection and read-only query access.
Safer for development/exploration (no write operations).
Source: https://github.com/LucasHild/mcp-server-bigquery

mcpServers._comment_database_toolbox: --- DATABASE-SPECIFIC MCP (via MCP Toolbox for Databases) ---

_comment: Google MCP Toolbox for Databases — unified MCP for Cloud SQL, AlloyDB, Spanner, Firestore.
Deploy as a Cloud Run service or run locally.
Provides: parameterized query execution with connection pooling and IAM auth.
Source: https://github.com/googleapis/genai-toolbox
Docs: https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/mcp-toolbox-for-databases-now-supports-model-context-protocol
Local setup (replace with your toolbox config path):
toolbox --tools-file=./toolbox-config.yaml serve --address=localhost:5000

_comment_coverage_gaps: --- GCP MCP ECOSYSTEM COVERAGE GAPS (as of March 2026) ---
Services WITHOUT dedicated MCP servers — use gcloud-mcp or Bash tool with gcloud CLI:
gcloud composer environments run ENV --location=REGION trigger_dag -- DAG_ID
Pub/Sub (MCP planned but not yet released):
TIP: The gcloud-mcp server wraps the full gcloud CLI, making it the universal
fallback for any service without a dedicated MCP server. Prefer it over raw Bash
commands when possible, as it provides structured tool responses.
_comment_setup_checklist: 2. Set project: gcloud config set project PROJECT_ID
gcloud beta services mcp enable bigquery.googleapis.com --project=PROJECT_ID
(repeat for each managed MCP server you plan to use)
4. Create OAuth client for remote MCP servers:
Google Cloud Console > Google Auth Platform > Clients > Create Client
Add redirect URI: https://claude.ai/api/mcp/auth_callback (if using Claude.ai)
6. Remove unused servers from this config before deploying to engagement
7. Test connectivity: run a simple query through each enabled server
